questionText: A circular racetrack of radius 300 m is banked at an angle of 15°. The coefficient of friction between the wheels of a race car and the road is 0.2. The optimum speed of the race car to avoid wear and tear on its tyres is (Take tan 15° = 0.27, g = 10 m s⁻²)

Find the value of frictional forces between the blocks A and B and between B and ground. (Take g = 10 m s⁻²)
How large must F be in the figure shown to give the 700 g block an acceleration of 30 cm/s²? The coefficient of friction between all surfaces is 0.15.

A given object takes n times more time to slide down a 45° rough inclined plane as it takes to slide down a perfectly smooth 45° incline. The coefficient of kinetic friction between the object and the incline is :

The minimum force required to start pushing a body up a rough (frictional coefficient μ) inclined plane is F₁ while the minimum force needed to prevent it from sliding down is F₂. If the inclined plane makes an angle θ from the horizontal such that tan θ = 2μ then the ratio F₁/F₂ is :

A block is able to slide freely down an incline of inclination θ at a constant velocity. If it is projected up the same incline with initial speed v, it will return back after a time: ( Given μs > μk)
